en: You think you're the one picking things —
really, the algorithm is picking you.

zh: 同一杯咖啡、同一份外卖、同一双鞋，
en: Same coffee, same takeout, same pair of shoes —

zh: 别人花的钱常常只有你的一半。
en: the next person often pays half what you do.

zh: 不是你不够精——是你被「太懂你」的时代挑中了。
en: You're sharp enough — this age just knows you too well.

zh: 一杯咖啡，藏着一场信息差
en: One cup of coffee, one whole information gap

zh: 你打开斯达巴克斯小程序，
en: You open the Sidabakesi mini-app,
a latte runs you thirty-two,
add a topping and you're closing in on fifty —
this is the price of "convenience."
But the same latte,
search "proxy order" on Little Yellow Fish,
lands in your hand for eighteen yuan,

zh: 走的是同一条订单系统，
en: runs through the same order system,
stars stack, refunds work, complaints register.

zh: 你以为多付的那十四块，
en: You assume the extra fourteen yuan

zh: 是咖啡更香、奶泡更细、店员笑得更甜？
en: buys a better roast, finer foam, a sweeter smile from the barista?
It doesn't.

zh: 你付的是「我今天值得花三十二块」的那种感觉。
en: What you paid for is the feeling that "today I'm worth thirty-two yuan."

zh: 这种感觉是被设计出来的，
en: That feeling was engineered,
and it's the priciest thing on the menu.
And it's not just coffee —

zh: 某幸会员日、疯狂星期四，
en: Some-Luckin members' day, Crazy Thursday,
every one of them has someone running proxy orders,
all on the same logic:
you're not paying for the meal,
you're paying for the five minutes you didn't spend searching.

zh: 打开小黄鱼，每天都是会员日。
en: Open Little Yellow Fish — every day is members' day.

zh: 互联网这十年最值钱的东西，
en: The most valuable thing on the internet this past decade
has never been any one product —

zh: 是你不知道、而别人知道的那段信息差。
en: it's the gap between what you don't know and what others do.
The two scripts of the algorithmic age
Script one is called "hurry up."

zh: 直播间的倒计时永远在响，
en: Livestream countdowns never stop ticking,
"Three-minute flash, last three units."
Three minutes pass, the timer quietly resets,
"last three units" is always three units.
Stop and think for a second —

zh: 真正的稀缺会让你冷静，
en: real scarcity makes you calm,
because there's no time and no winning the fight;

zh: 伪造的稀缺只会让你冲动，
en: manufactured scarcity only makes you impulsive,

zh: 因为它要的就是你不思考。
en: because it needs you not to think.
The louder something pushes you to "hurry,"
the less it's the case that you need it —
it's the case that it needs you.
Script two is called "knowing you too well."
Scroll past the same item three times,
and the feed pushes it at you over and over;
hesitate five seconds without ordering,

zh: 下一条广告立刻给你打九折。
en: and the next ad slides in with 10% off.

zh: 这套打法的精髓不是「骗」，
en: The essence of this play isn't "trickery" —
it's "waiting" —

zh: 等你最累、最烦、最孤独的那一刻，
en: waiting for the moment you're most tired, most annoyed, most alone,

zh: 推一件不贵的东西到你眼前。
en: to slide a cheap little thing into view.
The algorithm doesn't lack patience —

zh: 缺的只是你的那一秒犹豫。
en: it only lacks your one second of hesitation.
So the real key to saving money
has never been learning a few tricks —

zh: 是别在情绪最低的时候打开 App。
en: it's not opening the app when your mood is at its lowest.

zh: 我们为什么愿意付「智商税」
en: Why we willingly pay the "idiot tax"
Buy an iPad,
and the counter will always ask —
"Add AppleCare? Only two thousand."

zh: 大多数人会犹豫几秒，最后加上。
en: Most people hesitate a few seconds and then add it.
Not because they really fear it'll break,
but because they fear "what if."

zh: 商家卖给你的从来不是机器，
en: What the seller is offering you isn't the device —
it's your anxiety about "what if."
The deeper the anxiety, the steeper the premium.
Take the same phone:

zh: 发布会当天三万人抢首批；
en: launch day, thirty thousand people scramble for first batch;
six months later, it hits Little Yellow Fish,

zh: 同款九成新，价格直接腰斩。
en: same model, like new, price cut in half.

zh: 你买新品多付的那一半钱，
en: The half you pay extra for new

zh: 是别人替你交完的智商税；
en: is the idiot tax someone else paid on your behalf;

zh: 别人卖二手少收的那一半钱，
en: the half the second-hand seller gives up

zh: 是替你过完了的新鲜感。
en: is the novelty they used up for you.

zh: 这个逻辑在生活里到处都是——
en: This logic is everywhere in life —

zh: 办一张两千块的健身年卡，
en: buy a two-thousand-yuan annual gym membership,
and you use it fewer than five times in a year.
It isn't that you're lazy —
it's that the moment you signed up,

zh: 你已经把「我会变好」买走了。
en: you'd already bought "I'm going to get better."
The card is just the receipt.

zh: 焦虑收费，是这个时代最成熟的商业模式。
en: Charging for anxiety is the most mature business model of our age.

zh: 你不是在花钱，是在花注意力
en: You're not spending money, you're spending attention

zh: 想一想你今天点开过哪些 App——
en: Think about which apps you opened today —

zh: 某宝、某东、某书、某团、某猫……
en: X-Bao, X-Dong, X-Shu, X-Tuan, X-Mao...

zh: 每个 App 推过来的，
en: What each one is pushing at you
isn't a product —

zh: 是「下一个你可能会想要的」。
en: it's "the next thing you might want."

zh: 你今天点开 App，本来是为了搜一件东西，
en: You opened the app today to search for one thing —
three minutes later,

zh: 你已经在刷「猜你喜欢」第二十屏。
en: you're already twenty screens deep in "For You."

zh: 你以为是自己控制不住，
en: You think you're the one who can't stop,
but really the app knows —

zh: 你这一刻最容易被「下一条」拽住。
en: this is the moment you're easiest to hook with "just one more."
It never asked you for money,

zh: 但拿走了比钱更贵的东西——
en: but it took something costlier than money —
this stretch of your attention.

zh: 而注意力，才是钱包的入口。
en: And attention is the gateway to the wallet.
You think you're the one spending money,

zh: 其实是先有人花了你的注意力，
en: but someone spent your attention first,
and only then did you spend the money.

zh: 所以最简单的省钱动作不是比价，
en: So the simplest money-saving move isn't comparing prices —

zh: 是去把所有 App 里的「为你推荐」开关关掉，
en: it's turning off every "For You" toggle across every app,

zh: 每个月清一次关注列表。
en: and clearing out your follow list once a month.
The first few days will feel off,
but a week in you'll notice —
the screen has gone quiet,

zh: 钱包和注意力，一起回来了。
en: and your wallet and your attention come back together.

zh: 五分钟，把决策权拿回手里
en: Five minutes — taking the decision back into your hands
The thing that really saves you money

zh: 不是某个 App、某张券、某个插件，
en: isn't some app, some coupon, some plugin —
it's a single action —
pause five minutes before you check out.
The gap between the algorithm and you
isn't that it's smarter than you —
it's that it's more patient than you.
It can wait all day,
wait for the five seconds you're weakest.
The only way you beat it

zh: 是给自己留五分钟的「无聊」，
en: is giving yourself five minutes of "boredom",
and letting the impulse digest itself.

zh: 这五分钟，问自己三个问题——
en: In those five minutes, ask yourself three questions —
First, will I still use this next month?
Second, do I need it,
or does it need me to buy it?
Third, if I don't order tonight,

zh: 明早起来我还想要它吗？
en: will I still want it when I wake up tomorrow?

zh: 答得上来再买，答不上来就关掉 App。
en: If you can answer, buy. If you can't, close the app.
Not getting played isn't "learning to defend" —

zh: 是把决策权从屏幕拿回手里。
en: it's taking the decision back from the screen into your own hands.
They say money is earned —
but slowly you'll come to see —
money is even more so saved,

zh: 是一次次不上当攒下来的。
en: saved up one un-played moment at a time.

zh: 这个被算法包围的时代，
en: In this age surrounded by algorithms,

zh: 信息差不是用来套路别人，
en: an information gap isn't for playing others —
it's for not getting played by them.
May every yuan you spend,
be a fully awake choice.
